title: archanthemis marschalliana subsp. marschalliana
intro: Archanthemis marschalliana subsp. marschalliana, also known as Marschall's chamomile or chamomile of the steppes, is a flowering plant belonging to the daisy family. It is a perennial herb used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine to treat digestive issues and inflammation. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antispasmodic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including soothing digestive problems, reducing anxiety and stress, and promoting relaxation. Archanthemis marschalliana subsp. marschalliana can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
